health_zone_table.basis: SitRep52 Table 2 (2026-07-05) transcribed + reconciled against the INSP PDF (post 25100, pdf sha256 437c86d14b06b9f31821c7aa9bf2701af6f5275a5ab4f655f5d74dec202a3c52, 12 pages), byte-verified against the live INSP upload.

reconciliation.note: Rows reconcile to official province/national totals: 1607 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified = 1624; all 521 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unattributed context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate: Ituri 168 analyzed / 60 positive (35.7%); Nord-Kivu 82 analyzed / 3 positive (3.6%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected and under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 63 positives over 250 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep52 published a fresh patient-movement table (Tableau 7): 646 in isolation (205 confirmed / 441 suspected) at 94.2% occupancy. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at 137.0% saturation. 20 recoveries (all Ituri) and 7 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (all Ituri) were recorded.
109 people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) are confirmed in Ituri cumulatively, of whom 34 died (31.2%) and 46 recovered.
highlights: 63 new confirmed cases including 8 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-05, in Ituri (60 including 15 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (3). Confirmed deaths rose to 521 (+15 vs SitRep51).

The 63 new cases came from Bunia (14), Mongbwalu (10), Nia-Nia (8), Bambu (7), Mangala (7), Nizi (6), Rwampara (5), Fataki (1), Rimba (1) and Nyankunde (1) in Ituri and Musienene (2) and Masereka (1) in Nord-Kivu.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-05; affected zones remain 36/104. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.
Response activities were stopped in the Bunia health zone by providers (strike).
Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 56.3%, led by Katwa (73.1%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%); Mongbwalu carries 51.7% (149 deaths for 288 cases).
laboratory: SitRep52 reports Ituri collected/analyzed 168 samples with 60 positive (35.7%).
Nord-Kivu collected/analyzed 82 samples with 3 positive (3.6%). Sud-Kivu collected 2 samples still under analysis.
publication_context: INSP SitRep 52 reports through 2026-07-05 (post 25100; WP publish 2026-07-07T18:09:24Z). The report date is 05 July 2026.
SitRep52 is the intermediate cadence between SitRep51 and SitRep53; the cumulative rose by 63 confirmed and 15 deaths over 2026-07-05.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone this cycle.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1477 of 1624 DRC confirmed cases (90.9%) and 439 deaths (CFR 29.7%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 927 alerts of the day were reported (26 J-1 + 901 new) and 824 investigated (88.9%); Ituri 58.8% (147/250), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (662/662), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (15/15).
135 suspected cases of the day were validated (112 living, 23 community deaths).
12412 contacts were under follow-up and 9624 seen (77.5%); Ituri 77.6%, Nord-Kivu 77.2%. 611 new contacts were listed (Ituri 538, Nord-Kivu 73); 8 became suspect; 144 completed 21-day follow-up.
At PoE/PoC, 135862 travelers passed (95.8% screened); 8 living alerts notified (all Ituri, 6 validated); no dead bodies intercepted this cycle.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 26; new alerts 901; total alerts of the day 927. Tableau 3 investigated 824 (88.9%).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 81.6% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases stay counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri), and this cycle 2 dead bodies were intercepted at Ituri PoE/PoC.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Tableau 4 (05 July): 12412 under follow-up, 9624 seen, 77.5%. Tableau 5 reports 611 newly listed contacts (Ituri 538, Nord-Kivu 73), 8 became suspect, 144 completed 21-day follow-up.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep52 Tableau 7 census 646 at 94.2% occupancy (Ituri 477 @89.3%, Nord-Kivu 148 @137.0%, Sud-Kivu 21 @47.7%); split 205 confirmed / 441 suspected reconciles to 646. Exits: 20 recoveries (all Ituri), 7 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (all Ituri).

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 57.4% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers, accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Mongbwalu +50 beds, HGR Bunia +60 beds, CTE Elikya), and sustain the clinical trial launched at CTE CME Rwampara
Improve contact follow-up (81.6% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams in affected ZS, clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog, and protect IPC/EDS teams after the CMC Kiharo attack

preserved_conflicts: SitRep52 headline is DRC-only (1624 confirmed / 521 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope snapshot adds the Uganda component (20/2/11) separately.
SitRep52 is the intermediate report between SitRep51 and SitRep53: +63 confirmed and +15 confirmed deaths vs SitRep51 for 2026-07-05. Ituri +60, Nord-Kivu +3.
SitRep52 carries an Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone this cycle; affected-zone count remains 36 of 104 (Ituri 24/36).
SitRep52 published a fresh Tableau 7 patient-movement table: 646 in isolation (205 confirmed / 441 suspected) at 94.2% occupancy; Nord-Kivu isolation at 137.0% saturation.
Response activities stopped in the Bunia health zone by providers (strike).

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (Uganda MoH, last reviewed 2026-06-21); carried forward as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1644 confirmed / 523 deaths / 284 recovered.
Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death (Miti-Murhesa).
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1); unstable small-number ratios.

review_notes: SitRep #052 (report date 2026-07-05; INSP post 25100; WP publish 2026-07-07T18:09:24Z). PDF sha256 437c86d14b06b9f31821c7aa9bf2701af6f5275a5ab4f655f5d74dec202a3c52, 12 pages, byte-verified against the live INSP upload.
SitRep52 is the intermediate cadence between SitRep51 and SitRep53. DRC headline 1624 confirmed / 521 confirmed deaths (CFR 32.1%); +63 confirmed and +15 confirmed deaths vs SitRep51.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1624 confirmed / 521 deaths: 1607 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, all 521 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Fresh Tableau 7 patient-movement: 646 in isolation (205 confirmed / 441 suspected) at 94.2%; Nord-Kivu 137.0% saturation. Recovered advanced to 273 DRC cumulative (+19; 20 recoveries in movement offset by carry).
Laboratory: Ituri 168 analyzed / 60 positive (35.7%); Nord-Kivu 82 analyzed / 3 positive (3.6%); Sud-Kivu 2 under analysis (250 analyzed / 63 positive overall, 25.2%).
Uganda anchor carried at 20 / 2 / 11 (last reviewed 2026-06-21); country-scope 1644 / 523 / 284. Response activities stopped in Bunia by a provider strike.
